reasoning: The SOC2 control CC6.3 focuses on managing access based on roles and responsibilities, emphasizing least privilege and segregation of duties. In Kubernetes, this directly maps to RBAC configurations. Ensuring no cluster-wide admin bindings, enforcing least privilege, and maintaining role separation are crucial to achieving these objectives. Additionally, restricting service account privileges ensures that automated processes adhere to the same principles.
title: CC6.3 The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data, software, functions, and other protected information assets based on roles, responsibilities, or the system design and changes, giving consideration to the concepts of least privilege and segregation of duties, to meet the entity’s objectives
